channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New Orleans New Year's Day ramming is the latest attack to use a vehicle as a deadly weapon
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police say a person who drove a pickup truck at high speed into a crowd of people celebrating the new year in New Orleans early Wednesday was hell-bent on creating carnage.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy the Baptist: Carter redefined ‘evangelical,’ from campaigns to race and women’s rights
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Before reaching the 1978 peace deal between Egypt’s Anwar Sadat and Israel’s Menachem Begin, Jimmy Carter managed months of intense preparation, high-stakes negotiations at Camp David and a field trip to the Gettysburg battlefield to demonstrate the consequences of war.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: The top 5 education issues of 2024
item.description: Universities and state education policies dominated in 2024 with a tumultuous year that saw massive protests over the Israel-Hamas war and lawsuits over the separation of church and state in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors say federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they’ve ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he is planning to attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Tuesday that he's planning to attend the funeral of former President Jimmy Carter. Asked about it as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump responded, "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alabama mother charged with the death of her 2 children, 1 year old's body still missing
item.description: An Alabama mother has been arrested and charged with the death of two children following an investigation by the 24th Circuit District Attorney's Office and the Alabama State Bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Psychedelic therapy begins in Colorado, causing tension between conservatives and veterans
item.description: Colorado becomes the second state to allow psychedelic therapy this week after two years of rulemaking.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hundreds apply for restitution for abuse suffered at Florida reform schools
item.description: Hundreds of people who say they suffered physical or sexual abuse at two state-run reform schools in Florida are in line to receive tens of thousands of dollars in restitution from the state, after Florida lawmakers formally apologized for the horrors they endured as children more than 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Russian and Iranian groups over disinformation targeting American voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States has imposed sanctions on two groups linked to Iranian and Russian efforts to target American voters with disinformation ahead of this year's election.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KRON4

item.title: Visiting the Big Apple? Prepare to pay more if you drive to busiest part of Manhattan
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York’s much-debated plan to charge drivers to enter the center of Manhattan is set to take effect Sunday, following years of bureaucratic and legal roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The killing of an 18-year-old Ohio woman was solved with DNA technology after 43 years
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A man who was shot dead last month as authorities attempted to serve him an indictment on federal gun charges has been identified as the killer of an 18-year-old Ohio woman in a case that had gone unsolved for 43 years, police announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Florida deputy identified as suspect in 1979 cold case, death of his wife: sheriff
item.description: A former deputy has been identified as the suspect in a 1979 cold case that left a 25-year-old store clerk dead, the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, Fox 5

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KRON4

item.title: Woman burned to death in NYC subway identified by police
item.description: The woman was set on fire and killed while likely asleep on a stationary train at the Stillwell Avenue subway station on Dec. 22, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: The Pentagon chief loses bid to reject 9/11 plea deals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A military appeals court has ruled against Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin's effort to throw out the plea deals reached for Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two other defendants in the 9/11 attacks, a U.S. official said.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch WWE's 'Raw' on Netflix: What to know before the Jan. 6 debut
item.description: After 21 years, WWE's "RAW" is leaving cable to join Netflix every Monday night beginning Jan. 6, meaning streaming is the only way to watch the longest-running weekly episodic show.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, Fox 5

item.title: Argentine judge charges 5 people over death of former One Direction star Liam Payne
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — An Argentine judge has confirmed charges against five people in connection with the death of Liam Payne, a former member of musical group One Direction. The judge has ordered preventive prison for two of them for having supplied Payne with drugs.
item.dc:creator: ALMUDENA CALATRAVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mother speaks out after Missouri father charged with killing his son on Christmas Eve
item.description: A Christmas Eve hangout turned deadly when a High Ridge father pulled a gun on his son and his son’s pregnant fiancé.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, Fox 5

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Charles Dolan, HBO and Cablevision founder, dies at 98
item.description: Charles F. Dolan, who founded some of the most prominent U.S. media companies including Home Box Office Inc. and Cablevision Systems Corp., has died at age 98, according to a news report. A statement issued Saturday by his family said Dolan died of natural causes, Newsday reported late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4
